title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Into the Darkness: The World of Cave Exploration
content.text: Below the surface of the earth lies a world that most people will never see. Vast caverns, underground rivers and formations of astonishing beauty exist in near-total darkness, accessible only to those prepared to crawl, squeeze and rappel their way through narrow passages. (37) [37] Los espeleólogos son quienes se aventuran en estos lugares remotos y desconocidos. [/37] Speleology — the scientific study and exploration of caves — combines the physical demands of extreme sport with the intellectual rigour of natural science. || The appeal of caving is difficult to explain to someone who has never done it. Part of it is the silence: underground, all surface noise disappears completely. (38) [38] La oscuridad absoluta y el silencio crean una sensación única de aislamiento del mundo exterior. [/38] Many cavers describe feeling profoundly calm once they have adjusted to the dark, as though the underground world operates on its own unhurried timescale. || Safety, however, demands constant attention. Passages that appear passable can narrow without warning, and water levels in underground rivers can rise with terrifying speed after surface rainfall. (39) [39] Por eso los espeleólogos nunca se adentran solos en una cueva desconocida. [/39] Experienced cavers always work in teams, carry multiple light sources and leave detailed route information with someone on the surface before descending. || Beyond the physical adventure, caves are scientifically invaluable. Their stable temperatures and isolation from surface conditions make them exceptional records of environmental change. (40) [40] Los depósitos minerales en las cuevas guardan información sobre el clima de miles de años atrás. [/40] Stalactites and stalagmites grow at predictable rates and incorporate chemical signatures that scientists can read like tree rings, reconstructing climates thousands of years in the past. || Caves also harbour extraordinary life. Many cave-dwelling species — fish, spiders, insects and crustaceans — have evolved over millennia in complete darkness, losing their pigmentation and, in some cases, their eyes. (41) [41] Estas adaptaciones extremas fascinan a los biólogos evolucionistas. [/41] The isolation of cave ecosystems means that species found in one cave system may be entirely absent from another just a few kilometres away. || The discovery of new caves continues at a remarkable rate. In recent decades, explorers have mapped systems in Borneo, Vietnam and Patagonia that dwarf anything previously known. (42) [42] Cada nuevo descubrimiento demuestra que todavía queda mucho por explorar bajo nuestros pies. [/42] For speleologists, the greatest cave may always be the next one — the one that has not yet been found.

paragraphs.A: Speleologists — cavers who combine adventure with scientific inquiry — are among the few who venture into these hidden realms.
paragraphs.B: Complete darkness and silence combine to create a sense of total separation from the world above.
paragraphs.C: These evolutionary adaptations have made cave fauna a rich subject of study for biologists.
paragraphs.D: Some cave systems are so large that teams of explorers have spent weeks underground without reaching their furthest extent.
paragraphs.E: This is why experienced cavers never descend alone and always follow strict safety protocols.
paragraphs.F: Each new discovery reminds scientists and explorers that much of the underground world remains unmapped.
paragraphs.G: Mineral formations in caves preserve a detailed record of past climate conditions.

questions.explanation: A presenta a los espeleólogos como los exploradores de estas zonas ocultas, definiendo su perfil antes de explicar la disciplina.
B describe la oscuridad y el silencio absolutos que refuerzan la sensación de aislamiento, encajando con la reflexión de los espeleólogos sobre la calma.
E introduce la norma de no descender solo como consecuencia directa de los peligros mencionados, enlazando con las prácticas de seguridad.
D anuncia el registro climático que guardan los depósitos minerales, preparando la explicación sobre estalactitas y estalagmitas.
C señala el interés biológico de las adaptaciones extremas de los animales cavernícolas, conectando con la observación sobre el aislamiento de ecosistemas.
F concluye que los nuevos descubrimientos muestran cuánto queda por explorar, cerrando el artículo con el espíritu aventurero del espeleólogo.
